questions.question: Why does a capstone matter when each lesson already had its own tests?
options: Composition is a separate property: pieces can pass unit tests but the integration deadlocks, the loss diverges, or the memory grows when they run together
questions.explanation: End-to-end tests catch wiring bugs that no piece tests in isolation: rendezvous, barrier order, gather contracts.

questions.question: What four invariants does the demo verify?
options: (a) loss runs to step 20 without NaN, (b) every rank's parameter norm agrees, (c) per-rank optimiser memory equals the ZeRO-1 formula, (d) the step-10 checkpoint reloads byte-equal
questions.explanation: Each invariant catches a different composition bug: NaN catches divergence, norm-agree catches sync drift, memory catches sharding bugs, checkpoint catches write atomicity.

questions.question: Why is the model a tiny GPT instead of an MLP?
options: GPT adds LayerNorm running statistics, embedding gradient shape, and softmax+cross-entropy edge cases that an MLP would hide
questions.explanation: MLP gradient sync was verified in lesson 77; the capstone needs a model with more edge cases to prove composition under realistic ops.

questions.question: What does self-terminating mean in this context?
options: Fixed step count (20), exit 0 on completion, no human in the loop; if any piece deadlocks the demo never returns and CI catches it
questions.explanation: A bounded run with exit 0 lets the test rig assert termination; while-True loops mask deadlocks.

questions.question: How does the demo combine DDP and ZeRO?
options: DDP-style broadcast at init for parameter sync, ZeRO-1's reduce_scatter+allgather replaces optimiser.step; DDP gradient allreduce is subsumed into ZeRO's reduce_scatter
questions.explanation: ZeRO-1 owns the gradient sync via reduce_scatter, so DDP's separate allreduce is not needed; the broadcast at init is the only DDP-specific call that survives.

questions.question: Why does production use wall-clock checkpoint cadence instead of step count?
options: Step time varies with sequence length and microbatch count; a 10-minute cadence catches the same compute regardless of model size
questions.explanation: The lesson uses step-based for clarity; production runs typically save every 5-15 minutes of wall-clock.
